rootCauseHypothesis:
The hosting page is rendering the loop ring visually, but the colored arc segments are not exposed as accessible interactive elements. This usually happens when the arcs are plain SVG decorations, are covered by another layer, or have pointer-events disabled, so the automated test can only see the non-ring controls (maker/checker/verdict/log) and cannot interact with the ring. As a result, the required detail-card behavior tied to arc clicks cannot be triggered. Underlying error: TEST FAILURE

The loop ring's colored arc segment cannot be interacted with — the required clickable arc segments are not exposed by the page's interactive elements, so the interaction cannot be performed and the detail card behavior could not be verified.

Observations:
- The page shows the loop ring as an SVG with label 'Loop ring: 1 iterations', but no clickable arc segments are present in the interactive elements provided.
- Only non-ring elements (maker, checker, verdict text, and iteration log entries) are listed as interactive; no ring hotspot is available to click.

recommendedFixTarget.rationale: Expose the loop ring arc segments as real interactive targets in the DOM, not only as painted SVG paths. Ensure each colored arc has a pointer-enabled element (e.g., <path>/<button> or an overlay with role='button', tabindex='0', aria-label, and onClick/onKeyDown handlers) so the test can discover and click it. If the arcs are currently drawn inside a non-interactive SVG/group or have pointer-events disabled, remove that restriction or add transparent hit areas aligned to the arc segments. Also verify the click opens the detail card for the selected iteration segment.
